title: NOC 53200 – Athletes | GTR Immigration
description: Canada is a global sports powerhouse, and NOC 53200 covers the professional athletes who compete at the highest levels. From the NHL to Olympic sports and professional soccer, Canada offers a sophisticated infrastructure for athletic excellence. International athletes can leverage their world-class achievements to secure Permanent Residency, often through specialized streams that recognize global-level talent.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level3Major Group53 – Occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group532 – Athletes, coaches, referees and related occupationsMinor Group5320 – Athletes, coaches, referees and related occupationsNOC Code53200

duties: Participate in scheduled practice and training sessions.
Compete in professional sports events, tournaments, and meets.
Maintain high levels of physical fitness and skill through rigorous training programs.
Represent the organization or country at international competitions.
Engage in promotional activities and media appearances.
employment_requirements: Demonstrated athletic ability in a particular sport is the primary requirement.
Several years of experience in amateur or junior professional sports are usually required.
Professional athletes must be under contract with a recognized team or sport organization.
salary_prospects: Note: Professional athlete salaries vary wildly based on league and performance; figures below reflect standard coaching/operational baselines for the sector where public data is available.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. While the number of professional spots is limited, the continued commercialization of sports ensures that positions for elite athletes remain available.
labour_market_demand: Demand is driven by professional sports leagues (NHL, CFL, MLS, CEBL) and national sport organizations (NSOs). Canada frequently recruits international athletes to bolster professional rosters and enhance the competitiveness of local clubs.
World-Class Facilities: Access to elite training centers and stadiums.
Safe & Stable Environment: Focus on athlete wellness and long-term career support.
Cultural Passion for Sports: Huge fan bases and sponsorship opportunities.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: Specifically for athletes who have competed at a world-class level or have been self-employed in athletics.
International-to-National (Global Talent): Specialized work permits that can lead to PR.
Express Entry: For athletes employed as part of a sports organization with a valid job offer.
